description: POST /v1/automations with Idempotency-Key — role: owner. Demonstrates config.event minting, revision advancement, idempotent replay, and the 409 fingerprint-conflict case. Matches v1-architecture.md §4.2/§5/§6 and openapi.yaml #/components/responses/IdempotencyConflict.

first_request.note: automation_id was omitted from the request body, so it was derived deterministically from the Idempotency-Key (SHA-256, hex, first 32 chars — v1-architecture.md §4.2). Concretely: SHA-256('create-nightly-build-2026-07-19') = 96b0d50209e2b3162c39996341373146fc19723280ed8502854a854b288c5c90, and the id is its first 32 hex chars, 96b0d50209e2b3162c39996341373146. space_revision advanced by exactly 1 (129 -> 130), same as a task.event or message.event would.

replay_same_key_same_body.note: Identical response, including the same revision (130, NOT 131) — no second config.event was minted. This is the 'safe to retry a lost 200' case.

body.error: idempotency key was already used for a different operation

replay_same_key_different_body_409.note: Same Idempotency-Key, different canonical-JSON fingerprint (name and every_seconds both changed) -> 409, mints nothing. The client must pick a fresh Idempotency-Key for a genuinely different operation.

patch_not_found_404.note: Existence is checked BEFORE minting — a PATCH/DELETE against a nonexistent automation must not burn a revision on a no-op config.event (v1-architecture.md §5). Caller here is the viewer device from join.json; PATCH/DELETE/POST-run are the automation routes viewers are allowed to call, POST-create is owner-only (v1-architecture.md §3).
